settings.question: When should I switch to winter tires in Calgary?
settings.answer: The Alberta rule of thumb is the 7°C rule — switch to winter tires when temperatures consistently drop below 7°C. In Calgary that typically means mid-October for installation and late March or early April for the swap back. Don't wait for the first snowfall; winter tire rubber compounds grip better than all-season rubber at any temperature below 7°C, even on dry pavement.

settings.question: What's the difference between winter tires and all-season tires?
settings.answer: Winter tires use a softer rubber compound that stays pliable in the cold, giving you shorter braking distances and better cornering grip. They also feature deeper tread blocks with sipes (tiny slits) designed to bite into snow and channel slush away. All-season tires are a compromise — they handle mild winter conditions but harden in extreme cold and don't perform as well as dedicated winter tires in Calgary's freeze-thaw conditions.

settings.question: Do I need to book an appointment?
settings.answer: Yes — booking online takes under 2 minutes and guarantees your time slot. Walk-ins are welcome when we have availability, but winter tire season (October–November) fills up fast. We recommend booking at least a week in advance during peak season to secure your preferred date and time.

settings.question: Do you offer tire storage?
settings.answer: Yes, we offer seasonal tire storage for customers who don't have space at home. Your tires are cleaned, inspected, and stored safely between seasons. Contact us for current storage rates and availability. Storage is extremely popular — book early to guarantee a spot.

settings.question: Will my TPMS light come on after switching to winter tires?
settings.answer: It can, depending on your setup. If your winter tires are mounted on steel rims without TPMS sensors, your system won't read pressure and the light may illuminate. We can install TPMS sensors on your winter rims so the system works correctly year-round. Ask about TPMS sensor installation when booking your winter tire swap.

settings.heading: Winter Tires Calgary
settings.subheading: Protect your family this winter. Expert winter tire installation and the best selection of winter tires in Calgary — Michelin, Bridgestone, Hankook, and more.
settings.pricing_intro: We carry a full range of winter tires to fit every budget — from everyday commuter tires to premium ice compounds. Michelin X-Ice, Bridgestone Blizzak, Hankook Winter i*cept, Nexen Winguard, and more. Every installation includes mounting, balancing, torque check, and a TPMS inspection.
